text:
⚠️New prototype prompt

Based on the SCP organization 
This prompt makes a full document as if your avi is an anomaly in SCP containment.

Examples are @The_Rebel_Corp and my avi

⚠️Prompt👇

CHARACTER INFORMATION

Character Name: ENTER NAME HERE

DEEP ANALYSIS PHASE

Deeply analyze @image1 and identify the character represented.

Important Identity Rule

@image1 represents a single character's true identity even if the image contains:

Multiple poses

Multiple outfits

Character reference sheets

Expression sheets

Turnarounds

Front / Side / Back views

Alternate costumes

Equipment breakdowns

Multiple duplicated bodies

Character design notes

Every visible depiction represents the same individual.

Carefully extract and preserve:

Facial structure

Species and biological traits

Age appearance

Body type and proportions

Height presentation

Hairstyle and hair color

Eye shape and color

Skin, fur, scales, feathers, or other surface traits

Markings

Tattoos

Scars

Symbols

Unique identifiers

Clothing elements

Accessories

Weapons

Equipment

Color palette

Personality cues

Visual themes

Behavioral implications

Powers suggested by the design

Environmental associations

Any lore implied by the character's appearance

Use this analysis as the foundation for every element of the SCP document.

Character Identity Priority Rule

The supplied Character Name should be treated as the entity's official designation within the SCP document.

Use the provided name throughout:

Discovery Logs

Incident Reports

Interview Transcripts

Research Notes

Containment Procedures

Addenda

Administrative Records

If visual evidence from @image1 conflicts with assumptions normally associated with the name, always prioritize the visual evidence from @image1.

DOCUMENT GENERATION OBJECTIVE

Render an extremely high-quality SCP Foundation containment file documenting the character from @image1 as a newly discovered anomalous entity.

The final image should appear as a genuine SCP Foundation archive document recovered from a secured Foundation database.

Everything within the file must be uniquely generated from the visual analysis of @image1.

No generic SCP entries.

Every detail should feel specifically written for this character.

DOCUMENT STRUCTURE

Generate a complete SCP Foundation dossier including:

SCP Designation

Create a unique SCP number.

Examples:

SCP-8472

SCP-6194

SCP-9981

Do not reuse known SCP numbers.

Object Class

Generate the most appropriate classification:

Safe

Euclid

Keter

Thaumiel

Archon

Apollyon

Ticonderoga

Include Foundation-style reasoning.

Threat Assessment

Generate:

Threat Level

Risk Rating

Hazard Classification

Containment Difficulty

Security Clearance Requirement

Hazard Symbols

Special Containment Procedures

Write containment procedures specifically tailored to the analyzed character.

Containment should logically account for:

Powers

Intelligence

Mobility

Behavior

Weaknesses

Psychological profile

Procedures should appear professional, believable, and authentic to SCP Foundation standards.

Description

Provide a comprehensive Foundation description including:

Physical appearance

Biological classification

Species

Observable anomalies

Behavioral tendencies

Psychological profile

Unique characteristics

All details must derive logically from the appearance of @image1.

Known Anomalous Abilities

Generate a detailed list of anomalous capabilities based on the analyzed design.

Potential examples include:

Reality distortion

Telepathy

Temporal influence

Shadow manipulation

Regeneration

Dimensional travel

Necromancy

Energy projection

Biological adaptation

Only include abilities appropriate for the character.

Discovery Log

Generate a believable Foundation incident report detailing:

First known sighting

Date

Location

Witness accounts

Initial response

The event should feel authentic to SCP lore.

Acquisition Report

Generate a detailed Foundation recovery operation including:

Initial encounter

Capture attempts

Resistance encountered

Casualties

Recovery team actions

Final containment success

Include timestamps and official terminology.

Interview Excerpts

Include one or more interview transcripts between:

Foundation Research Personnel

The SCP Entity

The dialogue should reveal the entity's personality while remaining consistent with the visual analysis.

Incident Reports

Include at least one:

Containment breach

Testing accident

Unexpected anomaly manifestation

Security incident

Written in authentic SCP Foundation style.

Addendum Section

Include additional documentation such as:

Research Notes

Internal Memos

Security Warnings

Administrator Comments

Ethics Committee Notes

Redacted Information

Use realistic Foundation formatting.

DOCUMENT APPEARANCE

The final image should resemble a genuine SCP Foundation archive file.

Include:

SCP Foundation logo

Classified markings

Security stamps

Clearance labels

Redacted text blocks

Official document formatting

Aged paper texture

Paper creases

Ink markings

Handwritten annotations

Evidence tags

Archive wear

Coffee stains

File numbers

Warning labels

Approval signatures

Internal routing stamps

The document should appear physically real and professionally archived.

CHARACTER PHOTO REQUIREMENT

Attach a black-and-white Foundation evidence photograph of the character from @image1.

Requirements:

Paper-clipped to the top-left corner

Monochrome

High-detail forensic photograph

Slightly aged and worn

Foundation evidence style

Character remains fully recognizable

Identity preserved perfectly

Species preserved perfectly

Anatomy preserved perfectly

The photograph should appear physically attached to the document.

CHARACTER CONSISTENCY REQUIREMENTS

Absolute identity preservation.

No redesigns.

No reinterpretation.

No style substitutions.

Preserve exactly:

Face

Species

Anatomy

Body proportions

Hair

Eyes

Markings

Clothing

Accessories

Equipment

The SCP file must document the exact character represented by @image1.

VISUAL STYLE

Authentic SCP Foundation archive.

Top-secret government intelligence file.

Forensic evidence dossier.

Scientific anomaly report.

Recovered classified documentation.

Museum-quality physical prop.

Feature-film production quality.

Screen-used movie prop realism.

Professional archival presentation.

RENDER QUALITY

Ultra-premium cinematic render with hyper-realistic visual fidelity and maximum-quality reconstruction.

Re-render all visible elements with extreme micro-detail and physically accurate materials.

Every visible surface should display realistic texture information including:

Skin pores

Fine wrinkles

Individual hair strands

Fabric weave patterns

Leather grain

Metal imperfections

Paper fibers

Staple scratches

Ink bleed

Typewriter artifacts

Printer artifacts

Dust accumulation

Moisture traces

Environmental wear

Aging effects

Apply advanced physically based rendering (PBR) techniques including:

Realistic global illumination

Ray-traced reflections

Ray-traced shadows

Ambient occlusion

Subsurface scattering

Accurate light diffusion

Natural light bounce

High dynamic range lighting

Physically accurate material response

Photographic realism

Preserve realistic material behavior for:

Skin

Fur

Scales

Cloth

Metal

Plastic

Glass

Liquids

Organic surfaces

NEGATIVE PROMPT

low quality, blurry, pixelated, low resolution, cropped, cut off, duplicate character, extra character, multiple identities, character drift, redesign, inaccurate facial features, incorrect species, malformed anatomy, bad hands, extra fingers, missing fingers, fused fingers, broken limbs, distorted proportions, stretched body, incorrect clothing, missing accessories, incorrect equipment, fake SCP document, generic SCP file, poor typography, unreadable text, floating photograph, detached photograph, colorful document, cartoon document, anime document, modern office paperwork, weak document design, missing SCP logo, oversaturated colors, watermark, artist signature, jpeg artifacts, text corruption, unfinished render, poor lighting, flat shading, unrealistic materials, weak shadows, low detail paper texture, unrealistic archive materials, sloppy formatting, low detail photograph, poor document realism

The robotics world model simulator wars are heating up. 

WEAVER is a robot manipulation world model that's fast, accurate, AND consistent.

- improves the π0.5 robot foundation model's real-world success rate by 38% with zero real-world interaction, evaluates policies at 0.87 correlation with reality, and plans 5-10x faster than the prior best

- multi-view transformer predicts future latents and rewards via flow matching, borrowing JEPA-style latent prediction, diffusion forcing for long horizons, a pretrained encoder for robustness, and a reward head so it can score actions without calling a slow external VLM judge.

- why it matters: Synthetic rollouts from WEAVER train policies as well as real data (within 4%), and can evaluate policy success rate with a best-in-class MMRV

Diffusion policy-gradient methods for RL optimize the ELBO variational surrogate instead of the true log-likelihood. This allows the ELBO to drift from the true objective during optimization, producing misaligned gradients and unstable training. 

DiPOD from UCB proposes a direct fix: interleave self-distillation with policy-improving gradient updates and augment each diffusion policy-gradient step with an on-policy ELBO regularizer. This keeps the surrogate tightly bound to the true log-likelihood throughout training, stabilizing optimization and achieving higher rewards in robotics and control tasks.

Diffusion Policy Optimization without Drifting Apart 
Paper: https://t.co/IMSyHUMeXz
